target: Timestamp Error tab ts-april-2026-update block: verbatim-quote and classification citation verification + surgical patches
summary: Fetched live methodology.json and claim_index.json from john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/ and diffed every quoted string and classification tag in the ts-april-2026-update block on the Timestamp Error tab. Timestamp-scope trio (what_ots_proves, what_ots_does_not_prove, claim_level_rule) is verbatim-accurate. All 8 WIN-level and pred-level classifications are correct. The four agent_rules are quoted with consistent drop/substitution errors (missing words, simplified wording) that a careful dome defender can exploit, and two of the five taxonomy meanings drop their subject ("A result", "The model statement is"). Patches below bring every quoted string into verbatim agreement with the live files and add a discreet provenance footnote naming the source commit and verification date.

verification_report.commit_reference: 6e06efb (per prior change records; live file carries last_updated 2026-04-17 matching)

quotes_with_mismatch.live: Do not assume that an OpenTimestamps proof alone makes every registry claim prospective.
quotes_with_mismatch.block_current: Do not assume OpenTimestamps proof alone makes every registry claim prospective.

quotes_with_mismatch.live: Separate discriminating claims from supportive and pending items before scoring the model.
quotes_with_mismatch.block_current: Separate discriminating claims from supportive and pending items before scoring,
quotes_with_mismatch.diff: block omits "the model" at end (replaces with comma)

quotes_with_mismatch.live: Carry the critical open problems forward instead of deleting them from the strongest evidence case.
quotes_with_mismatch.block_current: Carry critical open problems forward instead of deleting them from evidence,
quotes_with_mismatch.diff: block omits leading "the" and compresses "the strongest evidence case" to "evidence"

quotes_with_mismatch.live: Use review-response.html when a critique depends on archival prompt wording or older slogan-level page text.
quotes_with_mismatch.block_current: Use review-response.html when critique depends on archival prompt wording
quotes_with_mismatch.diff: block omits "a" before "critique" and drops "or older slogan-level page text"

quotes_with_mismatch.live: A result depends on unresolved instrumentation, raw-data access, or a remaining loophole.
quotes_with_mismatch.block_current: depends on unresolved instrumentation, raw-data access, or a remaining loophole

quotes_with_mismatch.live: The model statement is incomplete, under revision, or presently favors a competing explanation.
quotes_with_mismatch.block_current: incomplete, under revision, or presently favors a competing explanation
quotes_with_mismatch.diff: block drops "The model statement is" subject
lowercase style used for inline parenthetical; recommended tightening to preserve capitalization since block wraps each in quote marks

verification_report.additional_live_claim_note: claim_index.json also now contains pred-solar-009 classified as open_refinement — this is a NEW ninth classified item compared to what our block mentions, and it happens to be the only non-prospective_confirmed open_refinement entry the author has published. The block does not need to enumerate it (the point stands either way: zero confirmed WINs classified prospective_confirmed), but the decider may wish to note its existence in a future expansion since it is the cleanest publicly-disclosed adverse result in the registry.
verification_report.pred_eclipse_tier3_timing_verified: claim_index.json summary reads: "Prospective August 12 2026 three-tier eclipse protocol..." — the block's wording "pending prediction for the August 2026 eclipse" is accurate.

patches.location: Paragraph 1 ("What the author now says"), agent_rules[0] quote
patches.current_text: "Do not assume OpenTimestamps proof alone makes every registry claim prospective."
patches.replacement_text: "Do not assume that an OpenTimestamps proof alone makes every registry claim prospective."
patches.rationale: Live methodology.json agent_rules[0] is verbatim "Do not assume that an OpenTimestamps proof alone makes every registry claim prospective." The block's omission of "that an" is a direct misquote of a string we present inside quote marks. Fixing this closes the most exploitable citation gap on the tab.

patches.location: Paragraph 3 ("Using the author's own taxonomy..."), five taxonomy class definitions
patches.current_text: prospective_confirmed ("a threshold or directional claim was registered before the confirming data was pulled or released"), retrospective_structural ("an after-the-fact structural argument built from existing public data"), supportive_nonunique ("consistent with the model but also plausibly explained by standard physics"), pending_contested ("depends on unresolved instrumentation, raw-data access, or a remaining loophole"), and open_refinement ("incomplete, under revision, or presently favors a competing explanation")
patches.replacement_text: prospective_confirmed ("A threshold or directional claim was registered before the confirming data was pulled or released"), retrospective_structural ("An after-the-fact structural argument built from existing public data"), supportive_nonunique ("Consistent with the model but also plausibly explained by standard physics"), pending_contested ("A result depends on unresolved instrumentation, raw-data access, or a remaining loophole"), and open_refinement ("The model statement is incomplete, under revision, or presently favors a competing explanation")
patches.rationale: Live methodology.json claim_classes[*].meaning fields are capitalized sentences. The block wraps each in quote marks, so preserving exact text closes the "you misquoted my own taxonomy" retort. Two of the five (pending_contested and open_refinement) had their subjects dropped ("A result", "The model statement is") — those are substantive, not stylistic, differences.

patches.location: Paragraph 4 ("The counterpoint reading"), three agent_rules quotes
patches.current_text: The other three agent_rules — "Separate discriminating claims from supportive and pending items before scoring," "Carry critical open problems forward instead of deleting them from evidence," "Use review-response.html when critique depends on archival prompt wording" — describe good-faith auditing, not evasion.
patches.replacement_text: The other three agent_rules — "Separate discriminating claims from supportive and pending items before scoring the model," "Carry the critical open problems forward instead of deleting them from the strongest evidence case," "Use review-response.html when a critique depends on archival prompt wording or older slogan-level page text" — describe good-faith auditing, not evasion.
patches.rationale: Live methodology.json agent_rules[1], [2], [3] verbatim. The block had trimmed trailing clauses and article words, and replaced "the strongest evidence case" with "evidence" — presented inside quote marks, these read as direct quotation and the mismatch invites a citation challenge.

patches.location: End of Paragraph 3 ("Using the author's own taxonomy..."), after the "test of the methodology's good faith" sentence
patches.current_text: a test of the methodology's good faith will be whether WIN-035–WIN-039 (the author's own previously-declared prospective set) are tagged prospective_confirmed when classification is extended.
patches.replacement_text: a test of the methodology's good faith will be whether WIN-035–WIN-039 (the author's own previously-declared prospective set) are tagged prospective_confirmed when classification is extended. (Verbatim quotes and classification tags in this paragraph and the paragraph above verified against methodology.json and claim_index.json at last_updated 2026-04-17.)
patches.rationale: Adds the discreet provenance footnote recommended by the curmudgeon (SEC-timestamp-error-tab-apr2026.c1, hole #5). Names the source files and the verification anchor (last_updated 2026-04-17 matches commit 6e06efb referenced earlier in the block). Forecloses the "you misquoted the live file" retort by showing the diff was done.

holes_addressed: SEC-timestamp-error-tab-apr2026.c1 hole #5 (moderate): Citation-verification gap — four verbatim strings from methodology.json and eight WIN classifications trusted via source analysis, not directly verified against the live file. Now verified.

holes_deferred.deferred_reason: The prior EXP-170 rewrite already softened "the column under that header would still be empty" to "As of 2026-04-17, the prospective_confirmed column contains no confirmed WINs — only one pending prediction (pred-eclipse-tier3)" plus a falsifiable WIN-035–039 test. That addresses this hole; no further patch needed in this expansion.

The prior EXP-170 rewrite already inserted the sentence "The dome author's own earlier self-declared prospective set (WIN-035 through WIN-039) has not yet been classified in claim_index.json as of this writing; the partial sample is the author's curated strongest-current-targets set, not his prospective subset." This reconciles the two blocks; no further patch needed.

Not in scope of this citation-verification pass. Recommend a separate future expansion to add a clause engaging with registry_scope.display_counts_meaning / display_counts_do_not_mean. The live methodology.json has these fields: "display_counts_meaning: The displayed counts are bookkeeping for the current registry state. display_counts_do_not_mean: They are not a substitute for claim-by-claim prospective audit."
Out of scope for this verification-focused expansion. A future pass could swap "rhetorical" for "documentary" vs "cryptographic" where the distinction matters.
Already handled in EXP-170: block currently reads "can be force-pushed — detectable through GitHub server logs and public forks, but without leaving a verifiable trace on the Bitcoin chain."

Now verified: claim_index.json summary says "Prospective August 12 2026 three-tier eclipse protocol", which matches the block's "pending prediction for the August 2026 eclipse" framing.

new_evidence_added: Direct fetch of live methodology.json (V51.1, last_updated 2026-04-17) — all four timestamp_scope/claim_level_rule strings verified verbatim
Direct fetch of live claim_index.json (V51.1, last_updated 2026-04-17) — all 8 classification tags cited in the block confirmed
New ninth classified item (pred-solar-009 = open_refinement) documented for potential future expansion; not required for this patch

anticipated_objections.objection: You nitpicked a few missing articles and called it a patch — the substance of the quotes was preserved either way.
anticipated_objections.response: The substance was preserved, which is why the patches are small. But the block wraps every quoted string in quote marks, which readers and reviewers treat as a claim of verbatim accuracy. A dome defender who notices even one mismatched quote can use it to impeach the whole citation paragraph. Fixing this closes a live rhetorical weakness at zero argumentative cost.

anticipated_objections.objection: You are chasing the author's moving target — next week his file will say something different and these patches go stale.
anticipated_objections.response: That is a feature, not a bug: the patch names the source URL and the last_updated date (2026-04-17) in the new footnote. If the author edits the file, our footnote anchors the quotes to a specific snapshot the Wayback Machine and the OpenTimestamps status_history.json already capture. Future versions can re-verify in a fresh pass.

anticipated_objections.objection: You should also fix the rhetorical-vs-structural framing flagged in curmudgeon hole #4.
anticipated_objections.response: Agreed, that is worth doing — but it is an argument-shape change, not a citation-verification change. Bundling it here would confuse the patch history. Noted in holes_deferred for a future pass.

kernel_of_truth: The citation-verification curmudgeon hole was the right catch: when a block quotes an adversarial author's own words inside quote marks, even a one-word drift becomes a weapon the author can pick up. The author of methodology.json wrote careful, precise sentences — "Do not assume that an OpenTimestamps proof alone makes every registry claim prospective" is more measured than the block's "Do not assume OpenTimestamps proof alone makes every registry claim prospective" because the "that an" hedges against misreading the OTS proof as automatically invalid. Restoring his exact wording makes his concession read more fairly — which strengthens, not weakens, our critique because the rhetorical credit is more genuine.
tldr_patch_note: No TLDR change needed. The ts-april-2026-update block TLDR does not reference the specific quoted strings; the patches affect body text only. Section 1.4 TLDR and Part 8.5 TLDR are untouched.
